HACKS “If I Had To Start
All Over Again From
SCRATCH,
Here's The Exact
Page-By-Page
Funnel I'd Use...”
by RUSSELL BRUNSON | PG 3
INSIDE THIS ISSUE:
“3 Key Marketing
Strategies From
Disney World &
Universal Studios...
(That You Can Use For Your Own
Sales Funnels To Transform Cold
Buyers Into DIE-HARD FANS!)”
by HEATH WILCOCK | PG 12

to Access All of Your Bonuses

-- 2 of 20 --

Dear Funnel Hackers,
I get asked often, “If you were to start over again from scratch, which funnel
would you use, and why?” The answer to this question, for me, is simple.
I would start with a webinar funnel.
If you have heard the story behind how we started ClickFunnels, you know
that when we first launched on September 23, 2014, I tried to model other
successful software companies. We created a free trial offer inside of a funnel
and started the launch.
After a few days, we heard crickets. The first launch, Funnel #1, had failed.
So we went back to the drawing board and built a new funnel with a new
message and launched again. Funnel #2 was also a flop. So were Funnels
#3, #4, and #5.
We had spent SO MUCH time, energy, and money on this new project that I
didn’t want it to fail. But after so much failure, I was beginning to think that
what we were selling wasn't right.
About that time, one of my friends asked me to speak at an event he was
running, and he wanted me to sell ClickFunnels for $997 at the end of my
presentation. I told him that I’d love to speak, but the problem was that no
one was buying it, even with a free trial!
He laughed and told me that he didn’t care, and that I needed to figure it out,
because his people needed a funnel builder. So I went back to the drawing
board and created a presentation (based on the Perfect Webinar script,
which you can find at www.PerfectWebinarSecrets.com and inside the book
at www.ExpertSecrets.com). A few days later, I was on stage for the first time,
explaining to the world what funnels were!
MAR 2022
VIRTUAL REAL
ESTATE SECRETS

by Heath Wilcock | Pg. 12

by Russell Brunson | Pg. 16
Featuring Russell Brunson
Cofounder of ClickFunnels Behind The Scenes Look At
Our Evergreen Webinar Funnels
That Built ClickFunnels...
(And New Hacks That DRAMATICALLY Increase Sales!)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 20 --

4 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
After about 60 minutes, I transitioned to the pitch,
made an irresistible offer, and watched in amazement
as people started to run to the back of the room to buy!
It was so much fun to finally see people “get it.”
That night at dinner, with my partners who created the
software, I told them that our lives were about to change
forever, because now I knew how to sell ClickFunnels.
Over the next few days after we got home, we started
to build out our first webinar funnel, to generate leads
who I could give that same presentation to. We started
driving traffic and, in less than a week, I gave my first
virtual, online version of the presentation that had
caused the table rush. Within minutes of ending the
presentation, hundreds of new members had signed up!
Over the next 12 months, I did over 100 live webinars
promoting ClickFunnels, which built the foundation for
what we have today!
So when people ask me what I would do if I lost every-
thing and had to start over again, it’s simple. I would
write a webinar pitch, I would set up this simple funnel,
start driving traffic, and do live webinars two or three
times a week for at least a year.
After I had perfected the webinar sales presentation, we
started to build out simple, automated webinars that
would sell for me 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
I want to show you behind the scenes of that funnel,
what we did, and why. I’ll also show you some cool varia-
tions that we’ve added recently to help make our leads
become better than free! I’m hoping that this issue will
either convince you to create a webinar, or if you already
have one that’s working, that you’ll find two or three
things that will help to increase your conversions.
THE "WEBINAR"
FUNNEL CONTROL
by RUSSELL BRUNSON
After giving this presentation hundreds of times
over the years, I had a chance to speak at Grant
Cardone’s 10x event in Las Vegas, in front of more
than 10,000 people. During that presentation, I set
a world record for selling from the stage. We did
$3.2 million in sales within 90 minutes.
You can actually watch
this full presentation here
if you’d like:
Scan the QR code
After the event was over, I took my video of that
presentation and used it as the actual webinar
video. You could use a video of you giving your
presentation live, or you could also just use a
Zoom replay video from a presentation that
you’ve done in the past.
The registration page for this funnel was simple. No
video, no long copy, just a curiosity-based headline,
countdown clock, and a button to register to see
the presentation. After they click the button, a pop-
up comes up asking for their email address.
Scan this QR code
to watch the
presentation!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 20 --

5	MARCH LE T TER
After they have registered, they are taken immediately to the webinar presentation page. You’ll no-
tice a few key things that we did on this page that helped to increase the stick rate (keeping them on
the page), consumption rate (getting them to actually watch the full training or to read the sales let-
ter version), and order rate (getting them to actually buy). Let me walk you through the key elements.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 20 --

6 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
Getting Them To Push Play
Above the video, we have the speaker icon with
the text “MAKE SURE YOUR SPEAKERS ARE ON AND
CLICK PLAY. May take 10 seconds to load.” We have
this because oftentimes video files this large take
a while to load initially, and slow-loading videos
will add to your bounce rate, so I use this headline
as a way to keep them on the page until the video
hooks them.
Subtitles On The Video File
Even though it’s a 90-minute webinar, many
people are still watching your videos silently.
Having transcripts burned into the video will
dramatically increase your consumption rate.
Urgency And Scarcity
We set up the page to expire 48 hours after they
register, and we show how long they have left on the
countdown clock. This helps to get people to watch it
immediately, and also gives us the ability, in the email
sequence, to push the urgency and scarcity to watch
the presentation over the next two days.
Read Transcripts
I noticed that for many of Agora’s offers on their vid-
eo sales letters, when you would try to leave, would
pop up asking if you’d like to read the transcripts of
the video. When you’d click YES, it would take you to
a sales letter version of the presentation. I realized
that many people will NEVER sit through a 90-minute
video presentation, because that isn’t the modality
that they like to learn from, so we started adding this
text version, and a huge percentage of our buyers
came from people who “read” the full video! (I’ll talk
more about how we structure that page below.)
Video Spoiler Box
Under the video, we added in a “spoiler” box to show
people what was going to happen inside of the
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 20 --

7	MARCH LE T TER
presentation. These are used just like bullet points in a sales letter, where one bullet could be the thing that
gets people to buy. In this case, one bullet is often the thing that gets people to watch the full presentation.
Order Button
This button doesn’t show up until I make the actual CTA in the presentation, and then it appears
under the video.
From this page, there are only two CTAs that both eventually lead to the same place. For the readers, they
can click on “Read Transcript'' and they are taken to a sales letter version of the offer. (It’s 90% verbatim from
the presentation but cleaned up to read like a letter, with headlines, subheadlines, images, etc.). From the
sales page version, they are taken to the order form.
For those who watch the video, they can click on the order button and be taken directly to the order form.
This layout and process for a webinar funnel is my favorite. We’ve modeled it numerous times in dozens
of industries, and had amazing results each time.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 20 --

8 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
Multiple Versions For Maximum Market Penetration
After running my core webinar for years, I wanted to find a way to get more people into ClickFunnels. One
day we had the idea to have one of our top success stories, Kaelin Poulin, do a version of my webinar that
would tell her story, relate better to a different segment of the audience, but make the exact SAME offer.
We took the proven webinar funnel structure (the science) and weaved her hooks, stories, pictures, and full
presentation (the art) into the funnel and launched it with great success. You can see the page here.
Registration Page
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 20 --

9	MARCH LE T TER
Sales Letter Version And The Order Form
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 20 --

10 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
The two big takeaways I hope you get from this are…
This structure works for any presentation. Just plug it into the proven webinar funnel framework.
If you have a presentation that is converting but want to reach out to a larger audience, have one of your
success stories create a presentation that you can turn into a webinar!
SLOs And Segmenting On Registration
We have a few other cool tweaks that have helped increase the amount of how much money we can spend
on advertising. As you know, my favorite Dan Kennedy quote is “Whoever can spend the most money to
acquire a customer wins.” We do this by adding a self-liquidating offer, or SLO, on the thank-you page for people
to buy before they join us on the webinar.
By adding these SLOs into the funnel, we averaged over $10 in profit for each person who registered…
BEFORE THE WEBINAR STARTED. Oftentimes, this covered the entire lead cost! Other times it just helped
to get us closer to a break-even point before the webinar even started.
Let me show you how it works.
1. When they register, I ask them what is the #1 thing that is holding them back.
I do this because, as you know, I’ve
written three books: one that helps
people with their funnels (Dot-
ComSecrets), one that helps them
with creating information products
(Expert Secrets), and one that helps
them with traffic (Traffic Secrets). So
I find out what problem they have,
and then on the thank-you page…
•
•
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 20 --

11	MARCH LE T TER
I make them a special offer for the book
from this series that will actually solve
their #1 problem!
You can see the three different thank-you
pages in the images on the right.
After they opt in, I also segment them
based on their choice, and send them
different email sequences leading up to
the webinar.
The emails push people to these “indoctri-
nation” pages, where I tell them a story
to get them excited about the webinar.
Then I make another special offer for the
book that will solve their #1 problem.
This works well for me, because I have three
different books to offer them. But this could
work for you even if you only have one product.
You can figure out three problems that your
product solves, then customize your thank-you
page and follow-up emails to show how your
SLO product solves their #1 problem.
Webinar funnels are some of the most power-
ful funnels you can create. Spend time studying
this and other funnels you see online, and then
let’s launch your first or next webinar!
If you need help writing the script for the
presentation, I’d recommend picking up my
www.PerfectWebinarSecrets.com product for $7.
It will teach you how to structure your
presentation in a way that will get people
to buy!
3.

4.
5.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 20 --

12 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
VIRTUAL REAL
ESTATE SECRETS
SECRET #3
A Monthly Bonus Behind-The-Scenes
Look At The Marketing Sorcery We’re Us-
ing In Our Ecomm Funnels That You Can
Steal And Profit From Big-Time!
by HEATH WILCOCK
Hey, it’s Heathykins! I recently returned from taking
the family to Disney World’s Magic Kingdom and
Hollywood Studios, and Universal Studios Orlando—
so I’m exhausted and now need a “dad vacation” of
peace and quiet.
Now, we all know Dan Kennedy’s love of Disney and
the practices he’s adopted for his own businesses,
such as controlling the customer's entire experience
from beginning to end.
In that same vein, I thought it would be fun to give you
an update on how Disney and Universal have evolved
and what marketing strategies they are using today
that you can apply to your own online sales funnels for
more leads, more sales, and of course...more profit.
Without further ado, here are Heath’s...
“10... 5... 3 Key Marketing
Strategies From Disney
World & Universal Studios...
(That You Can Take And Use For Your Own Sales Funnels
To Transform Cold Buyers Into DIE-HARD FANS!)”
1. Use all 5 senses to capture full emotion.
Taste, smell, touch, hearing, and sight. I always say the
more you use these senses, the more likely you and
your brand will be remembered.
Example: On Transformers: The Ride 3D at Universal,
there’s a part where it feels like a missile is about to
hit you, and as it gets close to your face, you can
even feel the “heat” of the missile, which was
obviously simulated by some heater outside of
the ride.
The visual aspect of the missile coupled with the heat
did this crazy trickery in my brain, making it feel real.
Lots of rides do this now. They’ll combine the 3D
aspect with four-dimensional elements by either
squirting you with water, blowing a bunch of wind,
or even tickling the back of your heels to recreate
what it would be like if a bunch of rats were re-
leased into the building.
Marketing Takeaway: Heighten the senses on your
offer.
Analyze your offer...your copy...and your entire sales
funnel. How many senses are involved? Does your
product have a powerful scent that’ll hit them with
delight before they even taste it?
Does your product taste similar to a favorite dessert?
For example, instead of boring ol’ strawberry flavor,
why not make it “strawberry cheesecake” flavor?
Could your VSL benefit more from dramatic music
at the “discovery” portion? Or perhaps by switching
from live action to animation and back?
By turning up the volume on the senses available
within your offer, you can trigger more engagement,
and in turn, become even more memorable.
Side note: None of the rides called the 3D glasses
“3D glasses,” because that would remove you from
the full experience. Instead, they called them some-
thing like “safety goggles” for the Star Tours ride or
“battle glasses” for the Transformers ride.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 20 --

13	MARCH LE T TER
We all know they are 3D glasses, but that’s what we
call it in the “real world.” When you give it a new
name, it heightens and sells the overall experience.
Consider that when you want to differentiate your
offer. By giving your “mechanism” (as in the key that
makes your offer different) a new name, it makes
the entire offer NEW in the eyes of your customer.
(See issue #1 for the importance of “NEW.”)
End side note...
2. The journey is part of the experience.
While walking around The Magic Kingdom, I kept
thinking, “Disney isn’t the best at rides, but they’re
the best at making you WAIT for the rides.”
Take for instance what “waiting in line” looks like at
a bank...
Boring, right?
Now if you were to take that same type of line and put
it at a Disney park in front of a ride, it wouldn’t work.
You’d get whiners and complainers and it would feel
10x longer to get to the ride than it should.
Then once the ride was over, you would start to
wonder if it was really worth the wait (a dreaded
thought that Disney and Universal want to make
sure never enters your brain).
So what do they do?
They transform the boring “waiting in line” into
part of the experience that helps build up your
excitement for the actual ride.
For example: At the new Rise of the Resistance
ride at Galaxy’s Edge in Disney’s Hollywood Studios
(a line we waited FOUR hours for...I’ll explain my
mistake in a sec), the ride is technically 18 minutes
long—a groundbreaking feat when it comes to rides
that are normally only a few minutes.
What they DON’T tell you is what happens when
that 18 minute timer starts...
See, before you’re actually sitting in a cart and cruis-
ing through an Imperial Star Destroyer (and yes, it
was awesome), they first “prep you” by taking you
into different “waiting rooms” that are treated as
part of the experience.
But really, we were still technically “waiting in line.”
So by the time we were actually sitting in a cart with
the bar over our legs and speeding through the Star
Destroyer, we forgot all about the boring part of
waiting and were now part of the ride’s story.
Marketing Takeaway: When sending your custom-
ers through a “journey,” consider the parts that are
boring and/or “work” to the customer.
How could you dress up the boring parts to make
them more adventurous and memorable? How
could you get them to their “main goal” faster?
What I’ve seen work time and time again is sending
customers an extra “bonus” they weren’t expecting.
It can be a book, a t-shirt, or even a few extra pack-
ets of your product along with a handwritten note.
They just waited for a few days to get your package,
but by adding something a little extra, you’ll help
them forget about the wait time (not to mention
help decrease returns).
3. Use the lightning lane of upsells.
This is a CLASSIC marketing strategy that is worth
bringing up again and again.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 20 --

14 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
If you’ve been to any of the Disney parks, then you
know about their upsell, which is the Lightning Lane
pass that allows you to “cut in line” and enjoy some
rides sooner than if you were to wait with everyone else.
The catch is that you have to act fast. There’s real
urgency, because they only allow so many people to
use the Lightning Lane for specific rides during certain
times. And you can only use it ONCE for each ride.
We didn’t act fast enough when it came to Rise of the
Resistance. Thus our four-hour wait time...
The Lightning Lane can make all of the difference
when it comes to enjoying the park’s experience.
Let’s say the park is PACKED and it’s your FIRST TIME
visiting a Disney park. Imagine if you didn’t get
the Lighting Lane pass and so you only got to ride
maybe two or three rides...
Would you want to go back? Probably not.
This is a proven marketing strategy that still works to this
very day when it comes to upsells…
“Do you want to have [main benefit] even FASTER
so you can start enjoying [giant desire] right away?”
Marketing Takeaway: If you can promise results faster,
whatever those results may be—faster cash, faster
slimming, faster disappearance of wrinkles—then offer it.
Remember, most people enjoy doing nothing. If you
can get them close to doing nothing while heightening
their overall experience, they’ll gladly pay you for it.
Until next month, sweetums.
Your friend,
Heath Wilcock
P.S. Did you see the news?
Disney recently announced that they are building a
community called Storyliving, where residents can live
in a Disney-esque neighborhood, complete with per-
fectly manicured lawns and sing-songy mornings with an
always-blue sky. Look it up. It’s wild.
Can you imagine living next door to Gaston from
Beauty & The Beast, seeing him saunter out of his
house to take out the trash in the morning?
Or have to get after your neighbors the Seven Dwarves
for throwing another late-night kegger? Crazy...
CLASSIC
KENNEDY
Help! I Can’t Get Out Of
The Box I Put Myself In!
The fast food industry got the idea for drive-in windows
from banks. I guess there was a McDonald's executive
sitting at the bank drive-through one day who thought,
“I don’t think we can fit the milkshakes in these tubes,
but…” NetJets, the leader in fractional jet ownership,
now owned by Warren Buffet, owes its birth to the
vacation time-share industry. The microwave in your
kitchen was not originally intended to go there; its
original manufacturer, Litton, believed no consumer
would buy it and built them only for restaurants. When
was the last time you heard of Litton?
What does this tell you? That successful businesses live
or die by cross-industry “borrowing” of ideas. That inspi-
ration more often comes from outside the box than from
within. Ordinary businesses stay ordinary, their owners
eking out only ordinary incomes—and working too hard
for them—as long as those owners foolishly and stub-
bornly, mentally stay in their own tiny backyards.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 20 --

15	MARCH LE T TER
Breakthroughs come from bringing in fresh ideas
found outside one’s own business and applying
them in new ways. You choose to limit or expand
your income by the way you reject or embrace ideas
found far afield from your present modus operandi
and industry norms.
The vast majority of ordinary business people
with ordinary incomes and never-ending ordinary
complaints about how hard they work but how
little they gain, about being unable to compete
with the bigger and cheaper, have this in common:
they get their hands on powerful information (like
that in this very publication) and waste their time
and energy in the noncreative activity of finding all
the ways it can’t and doesn’t apply to them. Some
people have such teeny, tiny, calcified, crippled
imaginations that they can only appreciate an
example precisely matched to them.
“Oh, that won’t work for me because her place sells
pizza and I sell Chinese food. And hers is in a medi-
um-sized city and I’m in a small town. And it rains
a lot where she is, but it’s sunny here. You have to
show me an example from a Chinese restaurant in
a small town where it’s hot and dry.”
Fools stay stuck in the very limiting “But my busi-
ness is different” box, thereby negating the value
of 99% of every successful strategy, example, or
model they see or are presented with.
My client list is, fortunately, chock-full of people
who think in very opposite ways. They get rich
by finding the non-obvious opportunities. Living
creatively. Adapting tried-and-true winning strate-
gies from somewhere else to where they are. They
attack each issue of my newsletter, each book I sug-
gest to them, with yellow highlighter and bias for
action, not a closed mind. They are willing, even
eager, to “reimagine” their businesses, while oth-
ers have a Bilbo Baggins (from The Hobbit) attitude:
“[We] have no use for adventures…make you late
for dinner!”
Space here does not permit telling you such client sto-
ries. But one of the most successful marketing strate-
gies of all time is called “gift with appointment.” Today
it brings new patients into dentists’ offices, affluent
investors to financial advisors’ seminars, new home
buyers to developments and resort communities, and
is in play in hundreds of fields, helping to create mil-
lions of sales appointments every week.
To the best of my knowledge, it came from a woman
named Estée Lauder. I wonder how many people from
how many different fields ignored it for how long,
because they thought, “Nothing having to do with sell-
ing lipsticks and perfumes could possibly apply to MY
business. MY business is different.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 20 --

16 	BEHIND THE SCENES / MARKE TING SECRE TS LE T TER
SECRETS OF
SUCCESS
by R U S S E L L B R U N S O N
WHY Do You Want To
Accomplish Your Goal?
In Simon Sinek’s book Start With Why, as well as
in his TED Talk that has been seen over 54 million
times, he discusses the principle behind every
successful person and business. He says, “All of the
great inspiring leaders and organizations in the world
think, act, and communicate in the exact same way,
and it’s the complete opposite of everyone else.”
He then draws out what he calls the Golden Circle. The
inner circle says WHY, around that is a larger circle called
HOW, and the biggest circle that encompasses both is
labeled WHAT.
He then goes on to say, “The way we think and act is
from the outside in. We go from the clearest thing—what
we want—to the fuzziest thing, WHY. But the inspired
leaders all think, act, and communicate from the inside
out, starting with the WHY.”
When I started my business, I unfortunately started
with my WHAT. I wanted to make money—that was
my external desire. During that period of time, it was
hard for me to get into momentum. I knew what I
wanted, and I was trying to learn the HOW, the pro-
cess that would help me to achieve the WHAT, but it
was hard to get passionate enough to make any lasting
progress. It was hard to wake up early, and it was hard
to stay up late. Everything I did felt hard.
About a year into this journey, my wife Collette and
I started our process to start our family. I’ve already
told you the story about her getting on fertility medi-
cation so we could get pregnant, but what I didn’t tell
you was the story about how we met our doctor.
I had been gone that day at school, and Collette was
working to support our new little family. During my
free time, I was learning about marketing and sales
and how to grow a company online. One day, after
Collette came home from work, she turned on an
episode of Oprah that had been recorded earlier that
day. During that episode, there was a segment about
a fertility doctor, who just happened to be located in
Boise!
When I came home, she was so excited to tell me
what she had seen. She also told me that we had an
appointment the next day! I remember telling her
how crazy it was that this doctor had made it onto
the Oprah show. As a new “marketer,” I knew that a
business's chances of getting on Oprah was less than
winning the lottery.
Over the next few months, we’d go into the clinic,
get the shots, take the tests, and pray for a miracle.
Month One…nothing happened. Month Two…noth-
ing again. On Month 3, our test was scheduled for the
day before my college graduation. Both of our families
were driving to our little house that day to celebrate
my graduation.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 20 --

17	MARCH LE T TER
As we were waiting for everyone to show up, the
phone rang. I picked it up, and honestly, I don’t
remember anything that the nurse said except
“Congratulations!”
Both Collette and I broke down crying, at the same
time as both of our families pulled up in front of our
house. She quickly looked at me and said, “You can’t
tell anyone. What if it’s not true? What if something
happens?” I agreed, and we went to greet our fami-
lies with red eyes that were still crying.
I’m sure they all thought something was wrong,
but after the hugs and the hellos, Collette looked
at me and silently mouthed the words “Okay, let's
tell them!” Within seconds, everyone was crying
because of the great news!
A few weeks later, we were back in the doctor's of-
fice for our follow-up appointment and ultrasound.
A few seconds after starting the ultrasound, the
technician said, “Whoa! There are two babies in
here!” I remember sitting there in shock just think-
ing about how blessed we were.
I then started to think about this doctor. What she
did wasn’t really a big deal to her. She had helped
THOUSANDS of people get pregnant. But she
had believed so much in her product, her service,
and her mission that she did the work to get on
Oprah. And because she had done that, my life was
changed forever.
As I started thinking more about it, I realized that
almost everything meaningful in my life had hap-
pened because some entrepreneur had risked
everything to bring their message to the world.
And that is when I found the WHY for my business:
I wanted to serve entrepreneurs.
Every entrepreneur has a product or a service that
has the potential to change someone’s life. While
I can’t do what these amazing creators can do, if I
can give them the tools and training they need to
get their message out to the world, then I could be
at least a small part of their journey to change the
lives of the people they have been called to serve.
That vision, that mission, that WHY gave me what
I needed. It created an insatiable desire to learn.
Sleep became a burden. Ideas for ways I could serve
my entrepreneurs started showing up everywhere.
From there, we created seminars and events, wrote
books, and created software, all with one goal: to
free entrepreneurs so they could get back to serv-
ing the customers they have been called to serve.
When I took my focus off of WHAT I wanted (to
make money) and focused on the WHY, more
money than I could have ever dreamed of started to
come.
As Simon Sinek said, “Inspired leaders all think, act,
and communicate from the inside out, starting with
the WHY.”
So my question for you is why do you want to
accomplish your goal? If you don’t have a strong
enough WHY, then it will be hard to stay on your
path and actually achieve what you are searching
for. It needs to become a pull—a constant, insa-
tiable desire for success that will keep you from
stopping. Something that you can’t shut off that
will move you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 20 --
